figures.caption: Table 12. Comparison of semantic segmentation performance on the ADE20K validation set. All methods utilize ResNet-101 as the backbone to ensure a fair comparison of architectural efficiency. DGM-Net achieves highly competitive results against established attention-based models.

Figure 8. Even under a constrained setting (Batch Size = 2), DGMNet maintains structural integrity. The red circles highlight the model’s ability to preserve slender poles and sharp boundaries, demonstrating that our geometric priors effectively stabilize the global context modeling of G-Mamba without requiring largescale GPU clusters.
Table 2. Quantitative results on Cityscapes validation and test sets. (B): models trained on both the train and validation sets. (C): models trained with additional coarse annotations (coarse+fine).

Figure 1. Overview of our proposed DGM-Net architecture. It follows an advanced encoder-decoder paradigm integrated with the Cascade Geometric Navigation SSM and partitions it into four primary stages to ensure structural consistency.
Table 1. The geometric and physical formulations of the priors generated by the DGM-Module.

Figure 5. Qualitative comparison on Cityscapes. Each row displays (from left to right): Input Mask, V-map, D-Map, Flow Field, and our Inference result. Our method consistently produces sharper boundaries across different scenarios.
Figure 6. Visualization results on Cityscapes val set.

Figure 7. It shows qualitative ablation of the cascade design. Compared to the single G-Mamba variant (middle), our full Cascade G-Mamba architecture (right) significantly reduces semantic fragmentation and produces more coherent predictions by progressively fusing global context with geometric priors.
Table 5. Ablation on Mamba/G-Mamba configurations. (All with DGM-Module).
Figure 2. Visualization of geometric priors: (a) V -map showing potential fields, and (b) Curv-map capturing curvature anchors.

Figure 3. Comparison of scanning mechanisms. (a) Standard Mamba performs an isotropic scan with uniform spatial priority. (b) Our G-Mamba adaptively modulates the scanning intensity T based on the geometric alignment Φdir .
